title: Protium tonkinense
taxonomy: Protium tonkinense is classified within the family Burseraceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the torchwoods or copal trees. It belongs to the genus Protium, which consists of various species of trees native to tropical regions. Within the Burseraceae family, Protium tonkinense is part of the order Sapindales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The family Burseraceae is further divided into several subfamilies, but the exact classification of Protium tonkinense within these subfamilies is not well-documented. However, the overall classification of Protium tonkinense as part of the Burseraceae family and the Sapindales order is well-established in botanical literature.

intro: Protium tonkinense, also known as the Tonkin elemi, is a species of flowering plant in the family Burseraceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Protium tonkinense include elemol, elemicin, and other terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
